themes.exampleQuote: A clearly expert chef. Classic sushi done with a modern style.
The price is on the higher end, but is justified by the quality of the food.
Busy, but that is to be expected.
For the service, I cannot give a positive review.
Really amazing drinks and dessert as well.

notable_quote: One of the best sushi experiences I have ever had overall — the quality of the food is excellent.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers reliably call out the kitchen's technical precision and ingredient quality, particularly the Hokkaido Uni Chirashi; the consistent friction points are pricing for casual visits and uneven front-of-house service on busy nights.
quarter_narrative: Mola spent Spring 2026 quietly becoming one of Pittsburgh's most-filmed restaurants — by everyone except itself. Twenty-five TikTok creators posted 30-plus videos tagged to the East Liberty spot this quarter, generating over 2.7 million plays while Mola's own Instagram posted twice in 30 days and published zero Reels. The restaurant leads the Pittsburgh Asian Kitchens family this issue on creator collaboration fit, the highest mark in that category across the group, which makes the gap between outside attention and in-house output the defining editorial fact of this cycle. Whether Mola consolidates that external energy or lets it keep cycling through other people's feeds will determine whether the #1 Sushi rank in Pittsburgh holds when the next issue closes.
tldr_read: Dominant creator coverage and a near-perfect conversion path, paired with a near-dormant Instagram the restaurant controls. Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Sushi.
tldr_meaning: The external signal is already doing the work of a much larger operation; the trajectory stalls if the business's own channels remain too quiet to catch any of it.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Pittsburgh Gave Mola 2.7 Million TikTok Plays. Mola Posted Twice.

playbook.headline: Post one Reel before this quarter closes
playbook.action: Instagram Momentum is the single weakest signal; zero Reels in 30 days and a 1.42% engagement rate are both recoverable with one well-framed kitchen or nigiri video.

playbook.headline: Tag back the creators already filming here
playbook.action: With 25 unique TikTok creators and 2.7 million plays this quarter, a simple reshare or story acknowledgment converts outside reach into a relationship the business actually owns.

playbook.headline: Address service friction in public responses
playbook.action: Review sentiment sits at 78 with a recurring service note — one consistent, specific public response to those reviews closes the gap that keeps an 80% five-star rating from reading as cleaner.
